observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations to:
a. Management should conduct a regular physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the RPCPPE thereafter, pursuant to the provision of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b. a periodic reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards maintained by the accountant with that of the Property Cards maintained by the General Services Officer should be regularly made to promptly detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be out rightly recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed that the municipal records be reconciled with that of GSIS to determine the exact amount of Due to GSIS account immediately remit the said amount, pursuant to Sections 4.1.2, 14.1 and 5 (c) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997.

We recommended that the municipal government should reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle the obligation immediately in accordance with Rule IV, Section 3 of Republic Act No. 9679, otherwise known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 or Pag-IBIG Fund. We also commended the Management for the effort of paying the remittances to Pag-IBIG.
We recommended the management to implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended that management adhere faithfully to the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 on RSSA in the submission of the aforecited reports. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, should exert extra effort to cope up with the deadline set for submission of said reports, so that delay will be minimized, if not totally avoided in the future.

1.7 Therefore, we recommended that the LCE direct:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct yearly physical count of all PPEs
owned by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular
No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 114 of MNGAS, for
LGUs, Volume I.
b. The Accounting Division and the General Services Division or the
Designated Property and Supply Officer to maintain the PPELC and
PC, respectively, and a periodic reconciliation be made to detect any
discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records and
reflected in the book of accounts.

2.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
record the transfer of the cost of completed projects booked as PPE
under the Trust Fund to the General Fund. Corresponding
depreciation expenses shall then be provided in the books in
accordance with PPSAS No. 17.

3.8 We recommended that the LCE instruct the municipal accountant to:
a. Reconcile their records with that of the GSIS to determine the exact
amount of Due to GSIS account and;
b. Immediately remit the said amount, pursuant to Sections 4.1.2, 14.1
and 5 (c) of the IRR of RA 8291.

4.3 We recommended that the LCE instruct the municipal accountant to:
a. Reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the
correct amount of the obligation and;
b. Settle the obligation immediately in accordance with Rule IV,
Section 3 of RA 9679.

5.9 We recommended that the Management implement the perpetual
inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with Section
114 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

6.7 We recommended the LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant to
record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated
accounting guidelines.

7.7 We recommended that management adhere faithfully to the provisions
of Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 on RRSA
in the submission of the aforecited reports. The Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer, need to exert extra effort to cope up with the
deadline set for submission of said reports, so that delay will be
minimized, if not totally avoided in the future.

8.9 We recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the implementing offices to expedite the completion
of the above-mentioned development projects;
b. Direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing
offices to ensure that only projects which are feasible and can be
completed within the year are included in the Annual
Development Plan; and,
c. Ensure that the implementing offices are provided with adequate
manpower and skills in the implementation of the 20% DF
projects.
We recommended the management to observe the limitations prescribed under Section 325(h) of the Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend to the management the following: a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997. b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower insufficiency of funds

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account amounting to ₱ 144,480.43 within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records with GSIS reiterated

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal government should reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle immediately.

1.5 We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take
the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real
Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card as the basis of the Municipal
Accountant in setting up the RPT/SET Receivable, and the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjustments upon collection, pursuant to Sections 20, 84 and 86 of
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

2.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee should
conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality, pursuant to the
provision of Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed the municipal records be reconciled
with that of GSIS to determine the exact amount of Due to GSIS account immediately
remit the said amount, pursuant to Sections 3.1, 4.1.2, 14.1 and 5 (c) of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as
Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997. We also commended the
Management for the effort of paying all of their GSIS remittances for the year
amounting to ₱ 3,792,291.60 during the year, thus enabling its officials and employees
in availing of the benefits and privileges offered by the GSIS to state workers.

8.4 We recommended that the municipal government should reconcile the records
with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle
immediately in accordance with Rule IV, Section 3 of Republic Act No. 9679, otherwise
known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 or Pag-IBIG Fund. We
also commended the Management for the effort of paying the remittances to Pag-IBIG.

11.8. We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to
settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to
Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009.
We recommend that the municipal mayor should instruct the municipal accountant to include the Motor Vehicle in the Statement of Financial Position and adhere to accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should provide the depreciation on all depreciable assets and adhere to Philippine Application Guidance Numbers 4, 5 and 6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).
observations.reason: Non-availability of records for some PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should include the above-mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement in accordance to paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

We recommend to the management the following: a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997. b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account amounting to ₱ 144,480.43 within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records with GSIS hence reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal government should reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle immediately.
We recommend that the municipal mayor should instruct the municipal accountant to include the Motor Vehicle in the Statement of Financial Position and adhere to accounting rules and regulations.

1.6. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Inventory Committee
to conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the
provision of Section 156 of Commission On Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992.

2.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should provide the depreciation
on all depreciable assets and adhere to Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) Numbers
4, 5 and 6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property,
Plant and Equipment (PPE).

3.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should include the above-
mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement in accordance to paragraph
8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant
and Equipment (PPE).

4.4 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to include the Motor
Vehicle in the Statement of Financial Position and adhere to Section 40 (b), Chapter 10,
of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for National Government Agencies
Volume 1.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed the municipal records be reconciled
with that of GSIS to determine the exact amount of Due to GSIS account immediately
remit the said amount, pursuant to Sections 3.1, 4.1.2, 14.1 and 5 (c) of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as
Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997.

6.3 We recommend that the municipal government should reconcile the records with
that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle
immediately in accordance with Rule IV, Section 3 of Republic Act No. 9679, otherwise
known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 or Pag-IBIG Fund

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to: a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Municipal Accountant to submit the BRS

observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records with GSIS implemented, hence reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal government should reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle immediately.

1.4. We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

2.5. We recommend to the management to instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the provision of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
3.7 We recommend that the municipal accountant should reconcile the municipal records with that of GSIS to determine the exact amount of Due to GSIS account and remit the immediately the said amount.
4.3 We recommend that the municipal government should reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle immediately.
5.4 We recommend that the municipal mayor should instruct the municipal accountant to include the Motor Vehicle in the Statement of Financial Position and adhere to accounting rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to: a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account amounting to ₱ 144,480.43 within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:

Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and

Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

We recommend to the management the following:
Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997.
PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.
Depreciation should be recognized by the Municipal Accountant on all depreciable assets under the PPE account.
We recommend that the municipal accountant should reconcile the municipal records with that of GSIS to determine the exact amount of Due to GSIS account and remit the immediately the said amount.
We recommend that the municipal government should reconcile the records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the obligation and settle immediately.

We recommend to the management to comply with rules and regulations on cash management and accounting standards that - 

	a)    The Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) as at   Year End and on a monthly basis, as provided under Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.	.

	b)  For the Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-125 A dated March 4, 1992.

We recommend that management adhere strictly to the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 on the Rules and Regulations on Settlements of Accounts in the submission of the aforecited reports. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, should exert extra effort to cope up with the deadline set for submission of said reports, so that delay will be minimized, if not totally avoided in the future.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Accountable Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the Cashbooks on Cash in Treasury, Cash Advances, Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the bank statements and subsidiary ledger, pursuant to Section 100 of P.D.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to make a journal entry on the reclassification of the account from the Land and Building to Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets and Construction in Progress-Building and Other Structures, pursuant to COA Circular No.2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 as shown below:

       Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets                  2,361,054.11 
       Construction in Progress-Building & Other Structures     1,781,660.20                              
                 Land and   Building                                                                            4,142,714.31                      

              To reclassify the Land and Building Account to Construction in Progress-
              Infrastructure Assets and Construction in Progress-Building and   Other  
              Structures.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to BIR amounting to ₱ 22,268.01 within the prescribed period, pursuant to paragraph 5 of BIR Form No.1601-C Monthly Remittance Return of Income Taxes Withheld on Compensation (Guidelines and Instructions).

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account amounting to ₱ 144,480.43 within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation(IRR) of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to PAG-IBIG account amounting to ₱ 37,796.27 within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No.9679, otherwise known as Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 or PAG-IBIG.
We recommend to the management to reconcile the Due to PHILHEALTH with a negative balance of ₱ 4,475.00 as of December 31, 2015 with the records of the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (PHIC).

We recommend to the management to submit the required reports within the prescribed period, pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

Recommendations:
We recommend to the management to comply with rules and regulations on cash management and accounting standards that -
a)  TheAccountant toprepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements  (BRS) as at   Year End and on a monthly basis, as provided under Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
b)For the Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-125 A dated March 4, 1992.

Recommendation:
We recommendto the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account amounting to ₱144,480.43 within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation(IRR) of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management adhere strictly to the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 on the Rules and Regulations on Settlements of Accounts in the submission of the aforecited reports. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, should exert extra effort to cope up with thedeadline set for submission of said reports, so that delay will be minimized, if not totally avoided in the future.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel assigned to submit the listing of real property tax declarants, amount and date due; and subsidiary ledger, pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspensions, pursuant to Section 9.4 of COA Circular No.2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
